texts.full_text: Q: MySQL keeps startIng logger and constantly restarts? Alright, I have updated version 5.1 to version 5.6 without uninstalling the old one by using updatE-alternatives. This works just fine on our production/master server, but our replication slave seems to be having trouble. First, in the processlist mysqld_safe just keeps starting the logger tool indefinitely until hundreds of instances are running and I restart the server manually. secondly, the server only runs for a couple seconds at a time without actually restartIt sounds like you may have encountered some issues with your MySQL update on one particular machine (the replication slave). Here's how to troubleshoot: 1. Check if there is any error in mysqld_safe logs located under /var/log/. If so, this could indicate a problem specific to that server which needs addressing before continuing further steps towards resolving these issues encountered during updates or installations of new versions such as 5-6 instead 2 . Ensure you have followed all necessary instructions for updating MySQL version (including uninstalling old one first) - if not done correctly it can lead multiple problems including those mentioned above 3. This balance is held remarkably well on Terrible Feelings' debut full-length Shadows. For those not familiar, Terrible Feelings are a quartet from Sweden (which may or may not add to their ability to be awesome, science is still in debate). The band specializes in hard driving, darkanthems that can be as fun to sing-along to as they are intense. Their debut full-length, Shadows, is a testament to their ability to balance these elements, creating a sound that is both dark and catchy. The album opens with the title track, "Shadows," a song that sets the tone for the entire album. It's a haunting, yet catchy track that showcases the band's ability to blend heavy, driving rhythms with introspective lyrics. The song's buildup, from the quiet intro to the full, aggressive crescendo, is a masterclass in tension and release. It's a song that demands attention and sets the bar high for the rest of the album. "Echoes" is another standout track, featuring a driving guitar riff that is both aggressive and melodic. The song's lyrics explore themes of isolation and loneliness, making it a standout track that is both emotionally resonant and musically satisfying. The control lines from the motion controller to the drive are connected through the breakout box. Connected to each drive is one servo motor. Commands and power between the drive and the motor are connected through a series of command lines. Encoders on the servo motor provide position feedback to the motion controller and are connected through a series of lines to the break-out box. Each axis in a motion control system typically includes a series of leach axis in a motion control system typically includes a series of linear or rotary actuators, such as motors, which convert electrical signals into mechanical movement. The servo motor is one common type used for precise positioning and speed control. Along with the actuator, each axis may also include: - Mechanical components: Such as gears, belts, screws (ball screw mechanisms), and linkages to transmit motion from the motor to the load. - Sensors: Including encoders or resolvers that provide feedback on position, velocity, and sometimes torque. These sensors are critical in a closed-loop system for maintaining accuracy and stability. - Bearings and guides: To support moving parts with minimal friction and wear, ensuring smooth operation over time. - Couplings: Used to connect the motor shaft to other components like ball screws or pulleys while accommodating any slight misalignments. These elements work together under the control of a motion controller that processes feedback from encoders and sends appropriate commands to each axis's drive amplifier. texts.full_text: your current solution is not applicable, let's maybe have a look at the problem from a different angle. I'm not a websocket expert but as far as I understand what you try to achieve with the asynch servlet calls is not possible. However, if you change the design/config of your project, this should be achievable. Personally I use Websockets to be able to send a message to an arbitrary user that did not necessarily made a request - as long as he is connected, he must get the message. To do this, I simply use the SimpMessagingTemplate class offered by Spring in their websocket support. To send a message to ANY USER THAT I WANT, I do this : @Autowired SimpMessagingTemplate smt; (.......) smt.convertAndSendToUser(recipient.getUsername(), "/queue/notify", payload); So in your case, you could, in your loop : make class instance method calls (instead of a servlet, no network transit, you cannot be faster ! Just a call to your biz-logic / service / whatever) every time a method returns data, use the SimpMessagingTemplate like in the snippet here above :) you can still do it asynchronously if you want ! :) By doing this, you reduce latency (calling servlets adds alot), and have a reliable technique. You can easily and quickly send thousands of messages to one user or to several users, at your own discretion, without stumbling upon the "10000 for BLOCKING" problem, which probably comes from more than 1 servlet "answering the same question" ;) To obtain a SimpMessagingTemplate from Spring, you will need to use the <websocket:message-broker> tag or the equivalent non-xml-java-config. When the setting tool (not shown) is actuated to set the anchor 12 the slips 16 and 18 tend to center the whipstock/billet 10 in the wellbore 14 as indicated by the annular gap 26 shown in FIG. 2 representing the set position. One reason this occurs is that the slips 16 and 18 are close to each other and move out at the same rates. Those skilled in the art will appreciate that a passage 28 is used for actuation of anchor 12 and after actuation leaves a passage that can provide access to the wellbore below the whipstock/billet 10. The problem with this design is that the whipstock/billet is a very long and slender structure that is subjected to complex loading when engaged by a rotating bit. When centered in a wellbore it has no lateral support and acts akin to a cantilevered beam subjected to torsion and lateral loads. As a result, grip failures have occurred in the past. The present invention addresses the issue with a grip system with offset grippers that initially push in a given direction laterally below a whipstock/billet so as to cant or cock the opposite end of the whipstock/billet against the wellbore whereupon that position is then locked in by another set of slips spaced from the initial slip or slips vertically as well as circumferentially, in the preferred embodiment. Additionally, the anchor of the present invention can support a downhole tool above and a tubular string below and be actuated to support both while also allowing fluid communication through the tool, such as a whipstock/billet or a billet, to the tubular string below. texts.full_text: e impossible to control. As mentioned above, the definition of intelligence as instrumental rationality is important here. (Instrumental rationality is the ability to select actions well in pursuit of a goal). But I’ll also note that the argument goes through even if we think this isn’t a good definition of intelligence. In that case, for the purpose of this question, we should just jettison the term artificial intelligence and call it artificial instrumental rationality instead. As argued above, the orthogonality thesis goes through for an “AIR.” Instrumental rationality is all that’s necessary for an agent to have superhuman ability to accomplish goals, so the relevant question is actually “Why wouldn’t it act morally by the time it gains enough instrumental rationality to be dangerous?” This phrasing is a bit of protection against eliding definitions of intelligence, and hopefully reinforces the relevance of the quoted arguments above. texts.full_text: запросов к БД, в идеале отправка обрабатываемых комманд. Я хотел бы узнать как это реализовать в теории. Но в примере: Клиенты C#. Сервер обрабатывающий запросы на Python. Примеры из java иРеализация взаимодействия между клиентами на C# и сервером на Python в локальной сети возможна через сетевые протоколы (TCP/UDP, HTTP) или с использованием готовых библиотек. Ниже приведены теоретические шаги и примеры кода для описанных сценариев. --- ### 1. Основы архитектуры - Клиенты (C#): Отправляют запросы (например, SQL-запросы, команды) на сервер. - Сервер (Python): Принимает запросы, обрабатывает их (например, взаимодействует с БД), возвращает результат. - Сеть: Все устройства находятся в одной локальной сети, сервер имеет фиксированный IP (например, `192.168.1.100`). --- ### 2. Варианты реализации #### Вариант 1: TCP-сокеты (низкоуровневое взаимодействие) C# клиент (TCP): ```csharp using System; using System.Net.Sockets; using System.Text; class Client { static void Main() { TcpClient client = new TcpClient("192.168.1.100", 12345); // IP сервера NetworkStream stream = client.GetStream(); string command = "SELECT * FROM users"; // Пример команды byte[] data = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(command); stream.Write(data, 0, data.Length); byte[] buffer = new byte[256]; int bytesRead = stream.Read(buffer, 0, buffer.Length); Console.WriteLine(Encoding.UTF8.GetString(buffer, 0, bytesRead)); client.Close(); } } ``` Python сервер (TCP): ```python import socket import threading def handle_client(conn, addr): print(f"Подключение от {addr}") data = conn.recv(1024).decode('utf-8') print(f"Получено: {data}") # Обработка команды (например, выполнение SQL-запроса) response = f"Результат для: {data}" conn.send(response.encode('utf-8')) conn.close() server = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)

texts.full_text: at different from previous shooters from Kenta Cho. Also as in previous shooters from Kenta, you can slow down your ship to dodge projectiles. But it takes only a few minutes of playing the game to discover a cool new feature: destroyed enemy ships leave weapon parts on the screen. You can grab/salvage these parts and stick them on to your ship. Once they become yours, they will fire in the direction that you stick them on. Very clever, innovative, and a lot of fun :) There seems to be no limit on the number of parts you can stick on, so after a while your tiny ship will grow into a behemoth. The more pieces you manage to stick on, the higher your score will be. You have shields to protect your ships, and in the latest version enemy fire no longer dislodges all the pieces from your ship. With excellent abstract graphics, addictive action, randomized elements, and the very cool "stick-on" feature, TUMIKI Fighter is yet another solid entry in Kenta Cho's growing repertoire of superb freeware gems. Watch out for more innovations from this author in the future - his reputation as master of SHMUP is well-deserved. Sightseeing tours It’s not hard to find something to do if you fancy a day away from the coastal resorts, and a trip into the interior to marvel at terrain of Timanfaya National Park is a well worth the time. This region was formed over 300 years ago and a sunset camel ride through the Fire Mountains will be an unforgettable experience. For panoramic views of the island and beyond you can climb to the Mirador del Rio viewpoint located at a height of 479 meters and used as a lookout point for pirates as far back as the 16thcentury. Or step back 4,000 years in time when taking a tour of the famous Cuevas de Los Verdes (the Green Caves), known as the longest network of volcanic tunnels in the world. texts.full_text: s a rapidly evolving field that harnesses the principles of quantum mechanics to process information. Unlike classical computers, which use bits as their smallest unit of data (either 0 or 1), quantum computers utilize qubits, where each qubit can exist in multiple states simultaneously due to superposition and entanglement properties inherent within atoms at extremely low temperatures—a state known colloquially as "quantum supremacy." The development of practical quantum computing has been a significant challenge because maintaining the fragile coherence time for these entangled systems is difficult. However, recent advances have shown that with proper error correction techniques it may be possible to achieve more stable and scalable qubits in future devices which could lead towards larger scale implementations capable enough at solving problems previously thought impossible by classical computers such as factoring large numbers or simulating quantum physics phenomena accurately—a capability known within the field of molecular modeling. One key tool for understanding these complex systems is a visualization called "Bloch sphere," an abstract representation in three dimensions used to illustrate how different states (represented on its surface) can be rotated according various types operations applied onto qubits, such as Hadamard gate or phase shift gates etc., thus giving us insight into the possible manipulations one could perform within quantum circuits leading towards computation. Dr. Smith's role on the national committee, which runs through August 2014, involves engaging in critical discussions on the economic impact of livestock and the associated public health concerns. His insights and feedback on these matters will be invaluable in shaping the policies and practices that guide the United States' approach to animal health and welfare. As the first Dr. P. Mikell and Mary Cheek Hall Davis Endowed Professor of Beef Cattle Health and Reproduction at MSU-CVM, Dr. Smith brings a wealth of experience and expertise to the committee. His research, teaching, and service endeavors have all been centered around improving the health and productivity of cattle, which is a key component of the American agricultural sector. The U.S. Agriculture Secretary's Advisory Committee on Animal Health is a prestigious body, tasked with providing expert guidance on a wide range of issues related to animal health and welfare. Committee members are selected based on their distinguished academic and professional contributions to the field, and Dr. Smith's appointment is a testament to his outstanding achievements and dedication to advancing animal health. Dopamine, acting through D1 and D2 receptors, promotes natriuresis and diuresis, in part by inhibiting Na(+), K(+)-ATPase activity, which decreases sodium reabsorption. Conversely, Ang II, acting through Ang II type 1 receptors, enhances Na(+), K(+)-ATPase activity, which promotes sodium reabsorption, and has an anti-natriuretic effect. Southern rapper Young Jeezy had made statements against the title of Nas' album, and also furthered his comments by questioning Nas' street credibility. They have since reconciled, with Nas appearing on Jeezy's 2008 single, "My President". Many other southern rappers such as Ludacris, Trick Daddy, and Big Boi (whose fellow Outkast member, Andre 3000, declared hip hop dead on 2001's "Funkin' Around", off Big Boi and Dre Present...Outkast) have also attacked Nas' album title claiming that it is targeted at Southern hip hop. Nas also has a fair share of supporters such as fellow New York rappers KRS-One, DMX, Raekwon, and Ghostface Killah. Reception Commercial performance Selling 355,880 copies in its first week, Hip Hop Is Dead joined It Was Written (1996) and I Am… (1999) as Nas's third album to debut at number one on the Billboard 200 chart. The title track "Hip Hop Is Dead" (produced by will.i.am), which contains samples from "In-A-Gadda-Da-Vida" by Iron Butterfly, and "Apache" by Incredible Bongo Band (which Nas previously used on "Made You Look", and Billy Squier's "The Big Beat", was the first single of the album. It received airplay on radio stations in Australia (Triple J), the UK, and in United States, notably on Hot 97. The single recently reached #48 on the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs chart and #41 on the Billboard Hot 100. The second single from the album Hip Hop Is Dead is "Can't Forget About You" (Featuring Chrisette Michele). It contains a sample from Nat King Cole's "Unforgettable". Critical response Hip Hop Is Dead received generally positive reviews from most music critics. At Metacritic, which assigns a normalized rating out of 100 to reviews from mainstream critics, the album received an average score of 79, based on 22 reviews, which indicates "generally favorable reviews". Nas is hip-hop's "grumpiest man", according to Jody Rosen for Entertainment Weekly, and the album "is a lot like Nas himself: impossible

texts.full_text: 0ISBN: ISBN-978-1-2671-7257-0ISSN: N/AThe Creation of a Culture of Inquiry: Engaging Teachers in Experiences with Art Objects in MuseumsMiller, StacyProQuest LLC, Ed.D. Dissertation, Teachers College, Columbia UniversityThe problem under investigation in this dissertation concerns teachers and their ability to continue to be lifelong learners through professional development initiatives in art museums. In this serial case study five teachers participated in a professional development program designed to engage them with art objects in museums, aiming to foster a culture of inquiry and lifelong learning. The study explored how these experiences influenced their teaching practices and attitudes towards professional development. Through a series of structured and reflective activities, the participants were encouraged to examine art objects not only as visual pieces but as catalysts for inquiry and critical thinking. The dissertation detailed the methods used, including museum visits, reflective journaling, and group discussions, to assess the impact of these experiences on the teachers. Findings indicated that the program successfully encouraged teachers to adopt more inquiry-based teaching methods, to view professional development as an ongoing process, and to value the integration of arts into their curriculum. The study also highlighted the importance of personal engagement with art as a means of rekindling teachers' curiosity and passion for learning. This research contributes to the field of art education and teacher professional development, offering insights into innovative approaches that can enhance teachers' instructional practices and their own

Secondary protagonists are also tricky because you don't want them to steal too many scenes from your protagonist, which can be difficult when they're interesting enough that readers would like more of him and may even wish he was actually a major viewpoint (which is not necessarily what as the author wants). This problem comes up most often in longer works or series.
